title: leucanthemum gaudinii subsp. barrelieri
intro: Leucanthemum gaudinii subsp. barrelieri, also known as the Italian daisy or Pyrenean daisy, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family, characterized by its white and yellow petals. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Leucanthemum gaudinii subsp. barrelieri include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it as a treatment for any health condition.
